reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal statement correctly: an arbitrary index type `ι`, a family of topological spaces `X : ι → Type*`, the nonemptiness hypothesis `∀ i, Nonempty (X i)` matching "each $X_\alpha$ is nonempty", the normality of the product `NormalSpace (Π i, X i)`, and the conclusion `∀ i, NormalSpace (X i)`. Mathlib's `NormalSpace` (defined in `Mathlib/Topology/Separation/Regular.lean` as "disjoint closed sets have disjoint open neighborhoods") is the standard ProofNet rendering of Munkres' "normal"; the same notion is used in both hypothesis and conclusion, so the logical content matches. The Π-type `Π i, X i` is the correct product space. The statement is the one Munkres asks to prove in exercise 32.2c.
